scenario: Harbor Retailer assembles audit trails on Camunda 8.8 across 8000 orders per day and stores per-step tags as a list of strings on the process instance, sometimes including null entries from an upstream supplier. A modeller needs the documented FEEL call from the list functions page that joins string entries, ignores nulls, returns null when a non-string non-null entry appears, and returns "" for an empty list.
question: What does string join(list) on the list functions page do with null entries, non-string non-null entries, and an empty list?

options.text: string join(list) replaces null entries with the literal text "null", returns a concatenated string for non-string non-null entries by coercing them with FEEL string(), and returns the literal "empty" on an empty list per the list built-in functions page

string join(list) raises an incident on the process instance when a null or non-string element appears in the list and never returns a string in those cases per the list built-in functions page on the FEEL conversion section

string join(list) ignores null entries, returns null if an item is neither a string nor null, and returns the empty string "" on an empty list per the documented behaviour on the list built-in functions page

string join(list) accepts only an exact list<string> argument with no nulls, returns null on a list containing any null entry, and returns null on an empty list per the documented signature on the list built-in functions page

a.text: Incorrect. The list built-in functions page does not document the substitution "null" for null entries or the literal "empty" for an empty list; null entries are ignored and an empty list returns the empty string "".

b.text: Incorrect. string join(list) does not raise an incident on null or non-string entries; the page states that null entries are ignored and that a non-string non-null entry causes the function to return null rather than a string.

c.text: Correct. The list built-in functions page documents string join(list: list<string>): string. If an item is null it is ignored, if an item is neither a string nor null the function returns null instead of a string, and the page shows string join([]) returning "".

d.text: Incorrect. The list built-in functions page states that null entries are ignored in the result string, not that the function returns null on any null entry, and string join([]) returns the empty string "" rather than null.

explanation: string join(list: list<string>): string joins a list of strings into a single string. If an item is null it is ignored for the result string. If an item is neither a string nor null, the function returns null instead of a string. The page shows string join(["a","b","c"]) returning "abc", string join(["a",null,"c"]) returning "ac", and string join([]) returning "".
